disclaimer: Campaign contributions do not prove quid pro quo. Correlation between donations and votes does not establish causation. This data presents publicly available financial and voting records for informational purposes.

organizations.name: AIPAC (American Israel Public Affairs Committee)
organizations.description: The largest and most influential pro-Israel lobby. It makes no direct candidate contributions itself but operates through its affiliated super PAC, the United Democracy Project (created 2022).

organizations.name: Democratic Majority for Israel (DMFI)
organizations.description: A pro-Israel organization operating within the Democratic Party, founded by Mark Mellman.

organizations.name: Republican Jewish Coalition (RJC)
organizations.description: Promotes Jewish Republican involvement and lobbied against the 2015 Iran nuclear deal.

organizations.name: Christians United for Israel (CUFI)
organizations.description: The largest pro-Israel organization by membership (10M+), founded by Pastor John Hagee; primarily grassroots.

organizations.name: Pro-Israel America
organizations.description: Bipartisan PAC that bundles contributions to pro-Israel candidates in both parties.

votingCorrelations.description: $26.4B in aid to Israel alongside Ukraine and Taiwan.
votingCorrelations.insight: Senators voting Yea had received an average of $1.24M in career pro-Israel contributions, compared with $235K among those voting Nay.

votingCorrelations.description: New sanctions targeting Iran's ballistic-missile program.
votingCorrelations.insight: Senators voting Yea had received an average of $890K, compared with $110K for the two voting Nay.

votingCorrelations.description: Would have required a 30-day withdrawal of US forces from Iran operations; defeated.
votingCorrelations.insight: Senators voting Nay (to continue operations) had received an average of $1.58M in career pro-Israel contributions, compared with $340K among those voting Yea (to withdraw).

keyFacts.fact: AIPAC-affiliated spending exceeded $100 million in the 2024 cycle, making the pro-Israel lobby one of the highest-spending interest groups in American politics.
The United Democracy Project spent roughly $14.5M to defeat Rep. Jamaal Bowman and $8.5M against Rep. Cori Bush in their 2024 Democratic primaries.
The US provides Israel $3.8 billion annually in military aid under the 2016 MOU — the largest bilateral aid package in US history ($38B over FY2019-FY2028).
Israel has received more cumulative US foreign aid than any other country since World War II — over $310 billion (inflation-adjusted) through 2024.
